data.task:
READ-ONLY owner-map atom (no behavior changes). Produce a concrete, evidence-backed map of who currently OWNS each governance authoring action and who merely transports it, so we can implement the SSOT + active-run-queue priority from a real map instead of guessing. Write the map to docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md (UPDATE the existing file in place; preserve any still-accurate content, correct anything stale, and date the revision). This is the ONLY file you may write; everything else is read-only inspection.

For EACH of these six actions, name the single current writer-of-record (file:function), then list every emitter/transport surface that calls or duplicates it, the edge cases it handles, and the tests that pin it:
  1. ticket create
  2. ticket close
  3. ticket repoint/reorder
  4. priority create
  5. priority edit
  6. priority archive

Minimum surfaces to inspect and account for (verify against LIVE code, do not trust the doc or ticket line numbers):
- Ticket create: packages/core/src/tickets/create.ts, compose.ts, loader.ts, index-helpers.ts; emitters packages/daemon/src/routes.ts, packages/cli/src/create-ticket.ts, packages/cli/src/run.ts, runner escalation packages/core/src/runner/triage.ts, and the create-ticket Play.
- Ticket close/repoint/order: packages/core/src/tickets/close.ts, repoint.ts, daemon close/repoint callers, ticket launch auto-close, cocoder/tickets/order.json, cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md mirroring.
- Priority create/edit/archive/order: core priority loader/composers, daemon priority create route and order route, requestAuthoringPlay, Oz chat author tool, CLI create/edit/archive wrappers, and the create-priority / edit-priority / archive-priority Plays.
- Active-run + commit seams: launchRun/inFlight guards, requestAuthoringPlay active-run refusal, atom verify/pass/fail commit sequence, Oscar support commit, wrap-up commit, run-end history commit, wrap audit, Deb status/watch projections.
- Dashboard/Oz visibility: ticket and priority read surfaces, pending/queued state projection, Oz chat confirmation text, behavior when a daemon is live but no run is active.

DELIVERABLES in the updated doc:
- A per-action 'single writer of record' line (file:symbol) with a one-line note where a SECOND copy of the file-format/INDEX/order/id-allocation contract currently exists (these are the duplications the priority must collapse).
- A short 'active-run refusal points' section: exactly where founder authoring is currently refused or silently held while a run is active (cite requestAuthoringPlay and any daemon guard, file:line).
- A 'pinning tests' list per action (test file paths) and an explicit 'UNPINNED' callout where a behavior has no test.
- A 'doc-vs-code drift' note: anything in the prior doc that was stale and you corrected.

ACCEPTANCE: the doc accurately reflects live code (every claim cite-able to a real file:symbol you actually opened); no product/runtime behavior changed; only docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md is modified. MUST NOT: edit any .ts, Play, prompt, or governance file; change any ticket/priority/order file. Report the exact list of source files you opened as evidence.

data.reason: Docs-only owner map; spot-checked load-bearing claims against live code — createTicket writes order.json (create.ts:66-96), Play step 10 omits it (real drift), requestAuthoringPlay refuses active run (launcher.ts:1923), and the central active-run Gap is accurate (createTicket/createPriority/reorderTickets routes lack the inFlight guard that only deleteWorkspace has at routes.ts:844). No runtime behavior changed; only docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md modified.

data.task:
Build the FOUNDATIONAL durable active-run governance-authoring QUEUE spine (schema-first), and wire exactly ONE producer (daemon ticket-create) as proof. Do NOT yet rewire every transport, hook the runner commit seam, build priority edit/archive core helpers, add the founder-decision contract, or close tickets — those are later atoms. Keep this atom tightly scoped and fully tested.

CONTEXT (from the run_246 owner map, docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md): 0063 has TWO failure modes that must collapse into one path — (a) requestAuthoringPlay (launcher.ts:1923) and CLI loop-down (run.ts:168-219) HARD-REFUSE authoring while a run is active; (b) the daemon routes createTicket (routes.ts:725), createPriority (684), reorderTickets (674) have NO inFlight guard and commit out-of-band during an active run, which wrap audit can flag as a mystery commit. The fix is a queue: while a run is active, ACCEPT the authoring request, persist it durably, return a visible queued receipt (with the reserved final id), project it as pending, and DRAIN it at a safe seam with a LEDGERED commit so wrap audit recognizes it.

BUILD in this atom:
1. A durable queued-authoring store (one new module, e.g. packages/daemon/src/authoring-queue.ts or packages/core as fits the existing seam — match how inFlight/run state is persisted). It must survive a daemon reload (persist to a file under the workspace/run state dir, same convention as existing daemon state). Each queued entry records: action kind (start with 'ticket-create'), workspaceId, the validated input, the RESERVED final id (allocate at enqueue time via the same nextTicketId rule so the founder can reference it immediately — the map flagged id-at-queue-time as preferred), an enqueue timestamp source that does NOT use Date.now() forbidden APIs in a way that breaks determinism (pass the clock in, mirror existing code), and a status ('queued' | 'committed' | 'error').
2. enqueueAuthoring(...) -> returns the durable receipt {queuedId, reservedTicketId, status:'queued'}.
3. listQueuedAuthoring(workspaceId) -> for pending projection.
4. drainAuthoringQueue(ctx, workspaceId) -> for each queued entry, run its DETERMINISTIC core op (ticket-create => createTicketCore with the reserved id), commit through the SAME governance spine (commitGovernance), append an audit/ledger entry tagged so wrap audit treats it as an expected governance commit (NOT an out-of-band mystery commit), and mark the entry 'committed'. On failure, mark 'error' with the message and leave it visible — never silently drop, never strand a half-written file.
5. Wire ONE producer: the daemon createTicket route (routes.ts:725). When ctx.inFlight.has(workspaceId) is true, ENQUEUE (return 202 + queued receipt) instead of committing immediately. When no run is active, keep today's immediate-commit behavior unchanged.

MUST NOT BREAK: existing behavior when no run is active (immediate create still 201s and commits). Do not change requestAuthoringPlay, the CLI guards, priority routes, or close/repoint yet. Do not touch git.

TESTS (add, and keep all existing green): cover (a) enqueue-while-active returns a queued receipt with a reserved id; (b) durability across a simulated reload (reconstruct store from disk, entry still present as 'queued'); (c) drain commits each entry via createTicketCore, the file/INDEX/order.json land, and a ledgered audit entry is appended that wrap audit does NOT classify as out-of-band; (d) no-active-run path is unchanged (immediate 201). Add a wrap-audit assertion (or extend an existing wrap-audit test) proving a drained queued commit is recognized, not flagged.

EXIT CRITERION: pnpm -w typecheck is clean AND the daemon + core test suites pass — run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` (and any wrap-audit suite you touched) and paste the real exit codes. WRITE-SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/**, packages/core/src/**, and their **/tests/** only. One-shot atom (design judgment required); not loop-amenable. Report the new module path, the routes.ts diff, the new test names, and the actual command outputs as evidence.

data.reason: Durable authoring-queue spine + ticket-create producer: enqueue reserves final id, persists atomically, drains via createTicketCore committing file/INDEX/order.json and ledgers the commit (store.recordCommitLink) so wrap audit's unledgeredWindowCommits does not flag it; errors stay visible. Ran it myself — typecheck clean, daemon 387/387, core 630/630 (incl. new authoring-queue, mutations-queue, wrap-audit tests). Scope confined to daemon/src+tests and core/tests; no-active-run path unchanged.

data.task:
Make the active-run authoring queue LIVE by draining it at the earliest SAFE commit seam, and expose queued entries so they are visible while pending. The queue spine + ticket-create producer already exist (packages/daemon/src/authoring-queue.ts; route enqueues at routes.ts createTicket when inFlight). Right now nothing calls drainAuthoringQueue during a run, so queued tickets never commit until... never. Fix that. Do NOT in this atom extend producers to priority create/reorder routes or requestAuthoringPlay/CLI, build the founder-decision contract, or close tickets — those are later atoms.

FIRST (read-only, ~10 min): map the daemon's run-advance seam. The drain MUST fire only at a STABLE boundary where no builder write is in flight and the governance commit cannot corrupt an in-progress atom — specifically (a) right after a per-atom verify PASS commit is recorded, and (b) after fail/rejected-atom handling is recorded, before the next Oscar directive is requested. Find the exact call site(s) in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts (the run loop that records atom commits / dispatches verify / requests the next directive) and/or the core runner seam it observes. State in your report the file:line where you hooked it and why that point is stable.

BUILD:
1. Call drainAuthoringQueue(ctx, workspaceId, <the same commitGovernance bound to ctx>, ctx.now ?? Date.now) at that safe seam, for the run's workspace. It must be a cheap no-op when the queue is empty (it already iterates only 'queued' entries — confirm no findWorkspace cost is paid pointlessly per atom if that matters; if it is heavy, guard with a fast emptiness check). Reuse the existing commitGovernance from launcher.ts — do NOT introduce a second commit lane.
2. Drained commits are already ledgered to the active run via recordCommitLink/recordEvent inside drainAuthoringQueue; confirm the run id resolved at the seam matches so wrap audit recognizes the commit. If a drained 'error' entry occurs, it must remain visible (status 'error') and must NOT abort the run or the builder atom — log/emit and continue.
3. Expose pending queued entries on a read surface so the founder can see them: add listQueuedAuthoring output to an existing daemon read projection (e.g. the workspace/run status route or listTickets response as a 'queuedAuthoring' field). API-level visibility only; do NOT build UI rendering in this atom.

MUST NOT BREAK: runs with an empty queue behave exactly as today (no extra commits, no perf cliff); the no-active-run immediate ticket-create path is unchanged; existing per-atom commit ledger/wrap-audit behavior is unchanged.

TESTS (add; keep all existing green): one END-TO-END daemon test proving the full lifecycle — start a run, POST a ticket while inFlight (gets 202 queued), advance the run to the safe seam (atom pass commit), assert the queued ticket file + INDEX + order.json now exist, the commit is ledgered to the run (listCommitLinks), wrap audit does NOT flag it (unledgeredWindowCommits-style assertion or the existing wrap-audit path), and listQueuedAuthoring no longer lists it as pending. Add a test that a drain 'error' entry stays visible and does not abort the run. Add/extend a test that the read projection surfaces a still-pending queued entry.

EXIT CRITERION: pnpm -w typecheck clean AND `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` green — paste real exit codes. WRITE-SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/**, packages/core/src/**, and their **/tests/** only. One-shot atom (seam placement needs design judgment); not loop-amenable. Report the seam file:line, the diff of the hook, the new test names, and actual command outputs.

data.reason: Drain wired at the correct stable seam — runner onSafeCommitBoundary fires after executeAgentStep's atom commit is recorded and before the next directive; launcher binds the existing commitGovernance (no second lane), empty-queue short-circuits, errors stay visible without aborting, queued entries surfaced on listTickets. End-to-end test launches a real run, queues mid-atom, and proves the drain commits at the boundary, ledgers to the run, and run-wrap-bypass-detected stays false (real wrap-audit recognition). Ran it: typecheck clean, daemon 389/389, core 630/630.

data.task:
Close the remaining out-of-band-commit hole: generalize the active-run authoring queue to also cover the two currently UNGUARDED daemon routes — reorderTickets (routes.ts:674) and createPriority (routes.ts:684) — so NO daemon governance route commits out-of-band while a run is active. The owner map flagged these two together as 'the Gap'. Do NOT in this atom touch requestAuthoringPlay/CLI refusals, priority edit/archive, the founder-decision contract, or ticket closures — later atoms.

BACKGROUND: the queue spine (packages/daemon/src/authoring-queue.ts) currently hard-codes a single action 'ticket-create'. The route enqueues when ctx.inFlight.has(workspaceId) and drainAuthoringQueue runs createTicketCore at the safe commit seam (already wired in launcher onSafeCommitBoundary). Extend this uniformly to two more deterministic actions.

BUILD:
1. Generalize QueuedAuthoringEntry into a discriminated union keyed by `action`: 'ticket-create' (unchanged behavior), 'ticket-reorder' (payload: the ordered ticket-id array), and 'priority-create' (payload: the CreatePriorityInput). Keep the persisted schema versioned; BUMP QUEUE_SCHEMA_VERSION and make readQueueFile reject the old version loudly rather than silently mis-parse (there is no production queue data to migrate). The receipt stays {queuedId, status:'queued'} plus action-appropriate identity: reservedTicketId for ticket-create, priorityId for priority-create, and a queuedId for reorder (no id). enqueue/list/validate must handle all three kinds.
2. SINGLE deterministic operation for priority-create (acceptance #1): the createPriority route (routes.ts:684) currently inlines the deterministic write (composePriorityMarkdown -> tmp write -> validateCreatedPriority -> rename -> registerLivePriorities -> commit files [priority.md, priority-order.json]). EXTRACT that write+validate+register logic (everything EXCEPT the commit and HTTP response) into ONE reusable function (e.g. createPriorityFiles in packages/daemon/src or core as deps allow) that returns the files to commit. The immediate route AND drain must BOTH call it — do not leave a second copy of the priority-create write contract. Collision/invalid-id/missing-Objective validation stays inside that one function.
3. drainAuthoringQueue dispatches by action: ticket-create -> createTicketCore (as today); ticket-reorder -> writeTicketOrder(ticketsDir, order) committing [tickets/order.json]; priority-create -> the extracted createPriorityFiles committing [priority.md, priority-order.json]. Each commits through the SAME bound commitGovernance, ledgers to the active run (recordCommitLink/recordEvent), appends the audit tag, marks 'committed'; on failure marks 'error' visibly without aborting the run.
4. Wire the two routes: in reorderTickets and createPriority, when ctx.inFlight.has(workspaceId), ENQUEUE (202 + queued receipt) instead of committing immediately; when no run is active, keep today's immediate behavior (call the SAME extracted op for priority-create). listTickets already surfaces queuedAuthoring; ensure reorder/priority-create queued entries appear there too (and/or the priorities read route if that is the natural home — keep it API-visible).

MUST NOT BREAK: no-active-run immediate reorder (200) and immediate priority-create (201) behave exactly as today, now through the extracted op; existing ticket-create queue behavior unchanged; per-atom drain still fires once at the seam and handles a mixed queue (e.g. a ticket-create AND a priority-create both drain in one boundary pass).

TESTS (add; keep all green): for BOTH new kinds — (a) enqueue-while-active returns a queued receipt and writes nothing/commits nothing yet; (b) durability across reload; (c) drain at the seam produces the real artifact (order.json reordered / priority file + priority-order.json) committed via the spine and ledgered, queuedAuthoring clears; (d) no-active-run immediate path unchanged; (e) a drain error stays visible without aborting the run; (f) a mixed queue (ticket-create + priority-create) drains both in one boundary pass. Reuse the real-run end-to-end harness from the ticket-create test for at least the priority-create lifecycle.

EXIT CRITERION: pnpm -w typecheck clean AND `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` green — paste real exit codes. WRITE-SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/**, packages/core/src/**, and their **/tests/** only. One-shot atom (discriminated-union + extraction design judgment); not loop-amenable. Report the extracted op's path, the queue union diff, new test names, and actual command outputs.

data.reason: Queue generalized to ticket-reorder + priority-create via a clean discriminated union (schema v2, old version rejected loudly); priority-create write logic extracted to one op (priority-authoring.ts:createPriorityFiles) called by BOTH the immediate route and the drain — no duplicated write contract, pinned by the updated orchestration-contracts test. Both unguarded routes now enqueue when inFlight; drain dispatches by action, ledgers, keeps errors visible without aborting. Ran it: typecheck clean, daemon 394/394 (+5 incl. real-run mixed-queue lifecycle), core 630/630; no-active-run immediate paths unchanged.

data.task:
Implement the MID-RUN FOUNDER-DECISION contract and pin the run_245 premature-wrap regression (ticket 0066). This is a Durable Orchestration Change, so START READ-ONLY (~15 min) and map the seam before editing.

READ-FIRST owner map (state findings in your report): in packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts (+ agent-step.ts, status.ts) find exactly how the loop currently handles (a) a normal atom pass/fail, (b) an 'awaiting-founder' wrap that ENDS the run, and (c) any existing 'surface a question but keep going' affordance. Identify where 'awaiting-founder' status is set, where the run loop terminates vs requests the next directive, and which RunStore events/status projections encode it. The defect (run_245 / 0066): a founder decision that could be answered mid-run while a concrete next atom remains was treated as a STOP condition, so the run wrapped prematurely and stranded follow-up (0065). Do NOT edit Oscar's persona prompt in this atom (packages/personas/base/** is Surface-A base governance handled in a separate atom with the portability lens); confine this atom to the runner contract + status mechanics + tests.

BUILD (from the map): add a runner-level distinction between two outcomes after an atom:
  - 'ask-founder-continue': a founder question is surfaced (recorded as an event + visible status the dashboard/Oscar can read) BUT the run does NOT terminate; when the next directive arrives (the founder's answer having been incorporated into the next delegate), the loop proceeds to the next atom WITHOUT losing builder context.
  - 'wrap-awaiting-founder': the existing terminal wrap that ends the run awaiting a founder decision (unchanged).
The mechanism must be minimal and live alongside the existing next-or-wrap handshake — a delegate directive that follows a surfaced founder question simply continues the loop; only an explicit wrapup ends it. Do not break the existing awaiting-founder wrap path, the max-atoms/max-rejects backstops, hold/stop/resume, or the wrap-up delivery sequence. Ensure any new status value is handled by status projections so it does not crash Deb/UI readers.

REGRESSION TEST (the core deliverable — pin the run_245 shape): add a runner test proving: atom 1 (preparatory) commits; a founder question is surfaced mid-run via 'ask-founder-continue' (run stays active, not wrapped); the next delegate directive (carrying the founder's answer) is accepted in-context; atom 2 is delegated and commits; and the run continues/wraps normally only AFTER the real remaining work — i.e. the run is NOT terminated at the decision point. Assert the run did not emit a terminal wrap at the decision boundary and that both atoms' commits are present. Also keep a test for the still-valid 'wrap-awaiting-founder' terminal path so the two are clearly distinguished.

MUST NOT BREAK: all existing runner tests (especially the awaiting-founder, hold/stop/resume, backstop, and wrap-delivery suites) stay green. No persona/Play prompt edits in this atom.

EXIT CRITERION: pnpm -w typecheck clean AND `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` green (paste exit codes); also run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` since status projections cross the boundary. WRITE-SCOPE: packages/core/src/runner/**, packages/core/src/** (status/store as needed), and **/tests/** only; do NOT touch packages/personas/**. One-shot atom (protocol design judgment); not loop-amenable. Report the seam map, the contract diff, the new test names, and actual command outputs.

data.reason: Mid-run founder-decision contract: new ask-founder-continue directive kind surfaces a question and continues the loop (records founder-decision-requested event, status awaiting-founder, run stays alive) vs terminal wrapup; directiveIndex decoupled from atom n so normal runs are byte-for-byte unchanged. Regression test pins the run_245 shape — prep atom commits, founder choice surfaced mid-run, answer accepted in next delegate, atom 2 commits, wrap only after, asserting NO wrap/run-end before the second delegation. Ran it: typecheck clean, core 632/632 (+2), daemon 394/394; no persona files touched.

data.task:
Reconcile the BASE orchestration governance so Oscar actually USES the new ask-founder-continue contract instead of prematurely wrapping (the durable 0066/F18 fix). The runner mechanism + dispatch text already exist (directive kind 'ask-founder-continue', buildNextOrWrapDispatch/buildFounderContinueDispatch in packages/core/src/runner/prompts.ts, verified last atom). What's missing: the STANDING Oscar persona/standards text still teaches 'founder approval needed' as a WRAP stop-condition, which is the premature-wrap habit. This is Surface-A base governance under packages/personas/base/** — it ships to every workspace, so apply the ADR-0012 portability lens and run the persona/Play tests.

FIRST (read-only, ~10 min) — owner map, ONE owner per concept: find every place the wrap-vs-continue / 'founder approval needed' rule is stated: packages/personas/base/** (the Oscar persona prompt and any shared standard like shared-standards.md / standards/*.md), and confirm how it relates to the runner dispatch text (prompts.ts buildNextOrWrapDispatch) which is the RUNTIME owner. Do NOT create a second contract: the runner dispatch already enumerates the directive kinds per-turn; the persona/standards text should teach the JUDGMENT (when a founder decision is mid-run-answerable with concrete next work, surface it via ask-founder-continue and CONTINUE; reserve terminal wrap-awaiting-founder for decisions that genuinely must end the run) WITHOUT restating the JSON contract a second time. State in your report which file owns the judgment rule and why.

BUILD (minimal, portable):
1. Update the single owner of the wrap/continue judgment in packages/personas/base/** so 'founder approval needed' is no longer an unconditional wrap trigger: distinguish 'ask-founder-continue' (mid-run-answerable, concrete next atom remains -> surface + continue) from terminal 'wrap awaiting founder' (the answer must end the run / needs a different launch/surface). Keep it role-teaching and repo-noun-portable per ADR-0012 — the text must still make sense with CoCoder-specific nouns stripped; if it cannot, it belongs in a workspace delta, not base. Do NOT restate the directive JSON shape (that's the runner dispatch's job) — teach only the judgment.
2. If the Oz/runner founder-facing wrap-up closeout contract (wrap-up Play) implies every founder decision ends the run, adjust only the wording that contradicts the new continue path — narrowly, without inventing a parallel format.

MUST NOT: change the runner code/dispatch (already done); duplicate the directive-kind enumeration into the persona text; touch product code outside packages/personas/base/**; broaden scope to 0065 run-dir or ticket closures.

TESTS: run the persona/Play content suites and any orchestration-contract guard — `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` (covers prompts.test, orchestration-contracts.test, priority-authoring-plays.test and persona content pins) and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`. If a persona/standards content test asserts the old 'founder approval needed -> wrap' wording, update that assertion to the reconciled rule and explain why in your report. If NO test pins this persona text today, ADD a minimal content assertion (in the core persona/standards content test) that the base text teaches the ask-founder-continue-vs-terminal-wrap distinction, so the fix cannot silently regress.

EXIT CRITERION: pnpm -w typecheck clean AND both suites green (paste exit codes). WRITE-SCOPE: packages/personas/base/** and **/tests/** only. One-shot atom (governance-judgment wording); not loop-amenable. Report the owner-map finding (which file owns the rule), the exact text diff, the portability justification (what makes it repo-noun-portable), and actual command outputs.

data.reason: Base-governance reconciliation completes the 0066 fix: oscar.md now teaches that a mid-run-answerable founder decision with a concrete next atom uses ask-founder-continue and continues, reserving terminal wrap-awaiting-founder for decisions that must end the run; wrap-up.md aligned (do not save a mid-run decision for closeout). Portable per ADR-0012 (judgment-only, no CoCoder nouns), one-owner (names the runner path but does NOT restate the JSON shape — runner dispatch stays sole contract owner), and pinned by a new content test asserting the old 'founder approval is needed -> wrap' wording is gone. Ran it: typecheck clean, core 633/633 (+1), daemon 394/394. Scope confined to packages/personas/base/** + tests.

data.task:
Implement the ADR-0027 §6 NESTED run-dir layout (ticket 0065): machine-local run dirs move from flat `local/runs/<runId>` to workspace-namespaced `local/runs/<workspaceId>/<runId>`, with a COMPAT READ-FALLBACK so existing flat dirs (including the currently in-flight run) still resolve. Do NOT in this atom physically relocate existing flat dirs on disk (that one-time migration is the NEXT atom and must skip active runs) and do NOT close any ticket. Read-first, then implement.

READ-FIRST (state findings): read cocoder/decisions/0027-workspace-storage-contract.md §6 + Migration outline (steps 5-6). The sole layout owner is packages/core/src/runner/run-dir.ts (localRunDir / localRunDirById). Enumerate EVERY consumer that builds or reads a run-dir path and whether it has a workspaceId at that point: the §6 list names daemon runsRoot in packages/daemon/src/server.ts, runner join(runsRoot, run.id) in packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts, runner IO in packages/core/src/runner/io.ts, the run-dir reader in packages/daemon/src/rundir.ts, and launcher pickup/nudge reads/writes in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts. Grep for localRunDir, localRunDirById, and raw `runs` path joins to catch any the ADR list misses. Report the complete consumer set and, for each, whether it has workspaceId (write path) or only a runId (needs the fallback resolver).

BUILD (run-dir.ts is the ONE owner — no second layout literal anywhere else):
1. localRunDir(runsRoot, {workspaceId, id}) -> join(runsRoot, workspaceId, id). This is the canonical WRITE path for any caller that has the workspaceId.
2. Add a resolver, e.g. resolveLocalRunDir(runsRoot, runId): string | null (or async), the COMPAT READ-FALLBACK for callers that only have a runId: return the nested dir if `<runsRoot>/<anyWorkspaceId>/<runId>` exists, else the legacy flat `<runsRoot>/<runId>` if it exists, else null. Keep it cheap and deterministic (no Date/random). This is what makes existing flat run dirs — and the live run — still load.
3. Repoint every consumer through the owner: write-path consumers (runner run-dir creation, launcher pickup/nudge writes, io) compute via localRunDir with the run's workspaceId; read-by-id consumers (daemon rundir reader, any launcher read that only has runId) resolve via resolveLocalRunDir with the legacy fallback. localRunDirById callers that lack workspaceId must switch to the resolver, NOT silently produce a wrong nested path.

MUST NOT BREAK: the in-flight run handshake — a runId that currently lives at the legacy flat path MUST still resolve (the fallback covers it); existing run-detail rendering for legacy dirs still loads; new runs write nested. Do not relocate any on-disk dir in this atom. Do not change portable `cocoder/runs/...` history layout (that is a different zone, already nested).

TESTS (add; keep all green): (a) localRunDir nests under workspaceId; (b) resolveLocalRunDir returns the nested path when present and falls back to the legacy flat path when only that exists, null when neither; (c) a run-dir read-by-id consumer (rundir reader or its unit) loads a run detail from BOTH a nested dir and a legacy flat dir; (d) a runner/launcher write goes to the nested path. Use a tmp runsRoot fixture; do not depend on real run data.

EXIT CRITERION: pnpm -w typecheck clean AND `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` green (paste exit codes). WRITE-SCOPE: packages/core/src/runner/**, packages/daemon/src/** (server, rundir, launcher), and **/tests/** only. One-shot atom (layout owner + fallback design); not loop-amenable. Report the consumer map, the run-dir.ts diff, every repointed call site, the new test names, and actual command outputs.

data.reason: ADR-0027 §6 nested run-dir layout: localRunDir is the single owner nesting by workspaceId; resolveLocalRunDir scans nested then falls back to legacy flat then null. All consumers repointed — runner creates new runs nested + resolves on resume, launcher resume-pickup/nudge and rundir reader and oz-context-pointer read-by-id via the resolver — so the in-flight run_246 legacy-flat dir still resolves (handshake safe). No write path uses the legacy fallback. New tests cover nesting, nested-preference, legacy fallback, null-miss, and run-detail load from both layouts. Ran it: typecheck clean, core 635/635 (+2), daemon 396/396 (+2). No on-disk dirs relocated (migration deferred to next atom).

data.task:
Complete active-run queue coverage for the last two deterministic ticket authoring actions: ticket-CLOSE and ticket-REPOINT. Today create/reorder/priority-create queue when a run is active, but close and repoint still REFUSE mid-run (CLI loop-down guard in packages/cli/src/run.ts; reconciliation guards). Extend the existing queue (packages/daemon/src/authoring-queue.ts) uniformly so a close or repoint submitted while a run is active is ACCEPTED + queued + drained through the governed deterministic op at the safe seam, exactly like the other kinds. Do NOT in this atom close any real ticket or do priority edit/archive — those are separate.

BACKGROUND: the deterministic single-writer ops already exist — core closeTicket (open->closed move, status, resolution, INDEX move, order prune, closed-ticket validation) and repointTicket (open-ticket priority mutation + matching INDEX priority update), with writeTicketOrder owning cocoder/tickets/order.json for drag-order. The queue is a discriminated union keyed by action with a drain dispatcher (applyQueuedEntry). The drain fires at onSafeCommitBoundary after each atom.

FIRST (read-only): confirm the exact signatures/return (files-to-commit) of closeTicket and repointTicket and how the daemon currently invokes them (close/repoint routes or reconciliation paths), and where ticket-close/repoint during an active run is currently refused. Report the call sites.

BUILD:
1. Add two queue kinds: 'ticket-close' (payload: ticketId + optional resolution text) and 'ticket-repoint' (payload: ticketId + target priority, plus any order change). BUMP QUEUE_SCHEMA_VERSION and reject older versions loudly (no production queue data to migrate). enqueue/list/validate/receipt handle them; receipt carries the ticketId.
2. applyQueuedEntry dispatches: 'ticket-close' -> closeTicket(...) committing its returned files; 'ticket-repoint' -> repointTicket(...) (+ writeTicketOrder if the order changes) committing the returned files. Same bound commitGovernance, ledger to the active run, audit tag, mark committed; on failure (already closed / missing ticket / etc.) mark 'error' VISIBLY without aborting the run.
3. Wire the transports: the daemon close and repoint routes/handlers ENQUEUE when ctx.inFlight.has(workspaceId) (202 + queued receipt) and keep today's immediate behavior when no run is active; the CLI loop-down close path (packages/cli/src/run.ts) must route a live-daemon close to the daemon queue path instead of a hard refusal (so a founder close during a run is accepted, not refused). Keep listTickets surfacing these queued entries (queuedAuthoring).
4. WRAP-TIME DRAIN BACKSTOP: ensure queued governance authoring cannot strand if it is enqueued after the last atom. If the existing onSafeCommitBoundary mechanism can be reused to drain once more at the wrapup boundary before the run ends, add that drain; if that is not cleanly reachable, instead make the daemon drain on run-end/wrap via the same drainAuthoringQueue call and add a test. Either way, prove with a test that an item queued with no subsequent atom still commits before the run finishes (or document precisely why it drains at the next run and surface it as still-pending — but do not let it silently vanish).

MUST NOT BREAK: existing create/reorder/priority-create queue behavior; no-active-run immediate close/repoint (today's behavior) unchanged; existing close/repoint/reconciliation tests stay green.

TESTS (add; keep all green): for BOTH new kinds — enqueue-while-active receipt + nothing committed yet; durability across reload; drain at seam produces the real governed result (closed ticket moved + INDEX + order pruned / repointed ticket + INDEX + order) committed + ledgered, queuedAuthoring clears; error visible without abort (e.g. close an already-closed ticket); no-active-run immediate path unchanged; the wrap-time backstop test from step 4.

EXIT CRITERION: pnpm -w typecheck clean AND `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/cli test` green (paste exit codes). WRITE-SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/**, packages/core/src/**, packages/cli/src/**, and their **/tests/** only. One-shot atom; not loop-amenable. Report the close/repoint call-site map, the queue/dispatch diff, the wrap-drain approach chosen, new test names, and actual command outputs.

data.reason: Ticket close/repoint now queue during an active run (schema v3, dispatch to closeTicket/repointTicket with open-state + missing-priority pre-checks, errors visible without abort); reconciliation close/repoint and the new daemon close/repoint routes and the CLI close all accept-and-queue instead of refusing/committing out-of-band; wrap-time drain backstop in attachRunLifecycle .finally (ledgers before inFlight.delete, errors swallowed safely) with a dedicated 'drains at wrap when no next atom' test. Reconciliation tests updated to the intended queueing behavior (202, nothing committed), not weakened. Ran it: typecheck clean, core 635/635, daemon 401/401 (+5), cli 50/50 (+1). Completes 0063's active-run-authoring scope.

data.task:
Create ONE follow-up ticket through the GOVERNED createTicket op (the single writer), tracking the deferred physical run-dir migration so the founder-listed 0065 scope item is not dropped. Do NOT hand-edit ticket files/INDEX/order.json; do NOT use `cocoder oz create-ticket` (daemon is live -> it would queue rather than create-now and you could not show the result this atom). Use the same approach as the close atom: a throwaway helper script in the scratchpad dir OUTSIDE the repo that imports createTicket from @cocoder/core and creates the ticket against cocoder/tickets, then remove the script.

The ticket:
- title: 'Physically migrate legacy flat local/runs/<runId> dirs to the ADR-0027 §6 nested layout'
- type: task
- priority: none  (the parent governance-authoring-ssot priority is completing; keep this in backlog, not pointed at a priority about to be archived)
- description (Context section): 'ADR-0027 §6 step 5 nesting landed with a legacy-flat compat read-fallback (run_246, localRunDir nests by workspaceId; resolveLocalRunDir falls back to the flat path). The one-time physical move of existing flat local/runs/<runId> dirs into local/runs/<workspaceId>/<runId> was intentionally deferred because §6 step 5 only permits the move "until no active run references the old shape" and run_246 itself referenced the legacy flat path during its run. Do the move once no active run references the legacy shape: relocate each flat dir using the run store runId->workspaceId map, skip any active/inFlight run, idempotent (already-nested untouched), then the compat fallback can eventually be retired. Reads are unaffected in the interim via resolveLocalRunDir.'

Note: createTicket allocates the next id by scanning open/ + closed/; with 0063/0065/0066 now in closed/ in the working tree, expect id 0067. Let the op allocate; do not hardcode unless it collides.

MUST: leave ONLY the one new open ticket file + INDEX.md + order.json changed by createTicket (on top of the already-pending close changes); do not modify product code; do not commit (the runner commits governance/support files at wrap). If createTicket reports not-created (id collision), STOP and report.

EXIT CRITERION: show `git status` proving exactly one new cocoder/tickets/open/NNNN-*.md plus the INDEX/order updates from this create (alongside the pre-existing close changes), the new ticket round-trips (status Open, full frontmatter), and run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` green (paste exit code). WRITE-SCOPE: cocoder/tickets/** only (plus scratchpad helper outside the repo). One-shot atom; not loop-amenable. Report the createTicket invocation, the allocated id, git status, and command output.
